message:
Sample app: live chat demo wiring up the three helpers

A small chat in web/chat.html demonstrating createClient + subscribeQuery
+ optimistic against a real WebSocket RPC server. Open in two tabs to
watch messages stream live across.

Pieces:
- web/chat.html — the page with form + log + a snippet of the actual
  client code visible alongside the running app, so what's-the-shape is
  obvious before the user opens DevTools.
- web/src/chat/main.ts — the client. ~120 lines including the rendering
  bits; the RPC shape is 5 lines (createClient → subscribeQuery →
  for-await render → optimistic on submit).
- web/vite-rpc-server.mjs — extended with a chat IFC + handlers. In-
  memory ring buffer of recent messages plus a fanout-on-post pubsub.
  Production would back this with a real broker; the point here is that
  the helpers don't change shape regardless of what's underneath.
- web/index.html — adds a new demo card linking to /chat.html.
- web/public/sitemap.xml + web/vite.config.ts — new page wired in.

Wire format inside the WebSocket frames is JSON for clarity. The
helpers don't care; a production deployment would use a Cap'n Proto
schema for the Message struct and skip the JSON encode/decode.

Build adds ~1.6 KB gzip for the chat page bundle. Total dev-server
upgrade-handler now routes /capnwasm, /capnweb, and /chat.
